2606.00807.title: Interaction-Centered Intelligence: Toward Interaction as the Primary Unit of Analysis in Co-Creative AI and Human-AI Systems
2606.00807.paper_summary: Traditional artificial intelligence has largely conceptualized intelligence as isolated computation occurring within bounded agents. Across classical AI, machine learning, and many generative systems, the dominant unit of analysis remains the individual model or autonomous system evaluated through outputs, benchmarks, prediction accuracy, or optimization performance. While these approaches have produced major advances, they often under-theorize the role of interaction in the emergence of intelligence, creativity, meaning, and adaptive behavior. This paper proposes interaction as the primary unit of analysis for co-creative AI and interaction-centered intelligence more broadly. Drawing from distributed cognition, embodied cognition, enaction, participatory sense-making, human-computer interaction, and computational creativity, the paper traces a historical progression toward increasingly relational accounts of intelligence. Building upon prior work in Creative Sense-Making, quantified co-creation, and co-creative systems such as the Drawing Apprentice and AI Drawing Partner, it argues that intelligence emerges through evolving interaction dynamics among agents, environments, and socio-technical systems rather than solely through internal computation. The paper introduces Interaction-Centered Intelligence as a framework for understanding human-AI co-creation, collaborative emergence, adaptive participation, and interactional dynamics. Rather than evaluating intelligence solely through generated outputs, the framework emphasizes interaction trajectories, coordination patterns, participatory engagement, adaptive regulation, and interactional drift unfolding through time. Implications for explainable co-creative AI, hybrid intelligence, enactive AI, and future human-AI systems are discussed.
2606.00807.paper_summary_zh: 傳統人工智慧在很大程度上將智慧概念化為在有限代理內部發生的孤立計算。在經典人工智慧、機器學習以及許多生成系統中，主導的分析單位仍然是個別模型或通過輸出、基準、預測準確性或優化性能進行評估的自主系統。雖然這些方法已經帶來了重大的進展，但它們往往對互動在智慧、創造力、意義和適應性行為的出現中的角色理論不足。本文提出將互動作為共創人工智慧和更廣泛的以互動為中心的智慧的主要分析單位。本文借鑒了分布式認知、具身認知、行動、參與式意義建構、人機互動和計算創造力，追溯了向日益關係化的智慧解釋的歷史進程。基於在創意意義建構、量化共創以及如繪圖學徒和人工智慧繪圖夥伴等共創系統的先前研究，本文主張智慧是通過代理、環境和社會技術系統之間不斷演變的互動動態而非僅僅通過內部計算而出現的。本文引入以互動為中心的智慧作為理解人類與人工智慧共創、協作出現、適應性參與和互動動態的框架。該框架強調互動軌跡、協調模式、參與性參與、適應性調節和隨時間展開的互動漂移，而不是僅僅通過生成的輸出來評估智慧。本文討論了對可解釋的共創人工智慧、混合智慧、行動人工智慧和未來人類與人工智慧系統的影響。
2606.00807.author: Nicholas Davis et.al.
